verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is classified as a soluble/secreted protein, in contrast to IZUMO1-3 which are transmembrane proteins. Multiple independent sources confirm it has no TM domain (5-1, 15-14) and is predicted to be secreted (12-5, 12-11). In sperm, it is likely an intra-acrosomal protein that is lost (not surface-exposed) after the acrosome reaction (1-14, 1-15, 6-9). In somatic tissues, the NCBI summary places it in the nucleus and HPA reports cytoplasmic expression in seminiferous ducts. No evidence of stable surface attachment to a TM partner, no CD designation, and no cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs exist. The protein body does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane by any mechanism.
